confusionGroups.name: Conditional: ば / たら / なら / と
confusionGroups.note: All four express 'if/when' but with distinct nuances: ば is hypothetical/general, たら is sequential or completed condition, なら is contextual/assumed condition, と is automatic natural consequence. Mixing them changes register, implication, and the relationship between clauses.

confusionGroups.name: Giving & Receiving: てあげる / てもらう / てくれる / てやる
confusionGroups.note: These four express favors between people but differ by direction (toward speaker vs away) and social register (neutral vs condescending). てあげる = speaker/in-group does for out-group; てくれる = someone does for the speaker; てもらう = speaker receives the favor; てやる = casual/condescending downward direction.

confusionGroups.name: Visual Appearance: そうだ(looks) / ように見える
confusionGroups.note: Both express a visual impression but differ in structure: そうだ(visual) attaches directly to verb/adjective stems, while ように見える uses a full predicate and emphasizes perceptual judgment. そうだ is more immediate; ように見える can involve more subjective interpretation.

confusionGroups.name: Hearsay & Inference: そうだ(heard) / らしい / ようだ / みたいだ
confusionGroups.note: All express 'it seems' or 'I heard' but differ in evidence basis and register: そうだ(hearsay) quotes second-hand information, らしい conveys evidence-based inference or rumor, ようだ is based on speaker's direct observation/reasoning (formal), みたいだ is the casual colloquial equivalent of ようだ.

confusionGroups.name: Causative vs Passive: させる / られる / させられる
confusionGroups.note: させる (causative) means the subject causes/lets someone act; られる (passive/potential) means the subject receives an action or is able to act; させられる (causative-passive) means the subject is made to do something against their will. Confusing させる with させられる reverses who controls the action.

confusionGroups.name: Honorific vs Humble: いらっしゃる / なさる / いたします
confusionGroups.note: Keigo (polite speech) uses different verb sets for talking about others (sonkeigo/respectful) vs oneself (kenjougo/humble). いらっしゃる and なさる elevate the other person's actions; いたします humbles the speaker's own actions. Using sonkeigo for yourself or kenjougo for others is a serious politeness error.

confusionGroups.name: Difficulty Suffixes: やすい / にくい / づらい
confusionGroups.note: All attach to the ます-stem to express ease or difficulty of an action. やすい means easy to / prone to; にくい means hard to (physical or practical difficulty); づらい means hard to (emotionally painful or uncomfortable to do). にくい and づらい are near-synonyms but づらい carries a more personal, uncomfortable nuance.

confusionGroups.name: Become / Make / Decide: ようになる / ようにする / ことにする / ことになる
confusionGroups.note: These four express change or decision but differ by agency and verb type. ようになる = gradual change into a state (no direct control); ようにする = make an effort toward a goal; ことにする = personal decision to do something; ことになる = an outcome decided by external forces or circumstances.

confusionGroups.name: Just Happened: たばかり / たところ / さっき
confusionGroups.note: All three refer to recent past events but differ in structure and precision. たばかり (just did, recency still relevant) focuses on freshness of the completed action. たところ (just finished, immediate past) emphasizes the point of completion. さっき is a standalone adverb meaning 'a moment ago' with no structural attachment.

confusionGroups.name: While / During: 間 / 間に / ながら
confusionGroups.note: All three involve two overlapping time spans but differ in focus. 間 means for the entire duration of A, B is also true (simultaneous ongoing). 間に means within the duration of A, B occurs (a separate event happened). ながら means two actions performed simultaneously by the same subject, emphasizing parallel activity.

confusionGroups.name: Negation Degree: なかなか〜ない / 全然〜ない / あまり〜ない
confusionGroups.note: All three intensify negative sentences but by different degrees. 全然〜ない = complete negation (not at all). あまり〜ない = partial negation (not very / not much). なかなか〜ない = contrary-to-expectation negation (try as one might, it just won't happen). Choosing the wrong adverb produces a different nuance of failure or absence.

confusionGroups.name: Must / Should: なければいけない / なければならない / はずだ
confusionGroups.note: These express obligation and expectation but differ in source and strength. なければいけない = must (personal/spoken obligation). なければならない = must (formal/impersonal, stronger). はずだ = should/ought to be (reasonable expectation based on logic or prior knowledge, not command). はずがない is the negative denial: there is no way.

confusionGroups.name: Te-Form Completion Nuances: てしまう / ておく / てみる
confusionGroups.note: All attach to the て-form but express different relations to the action's outcome. てしまう = completed fully (often with regret or finality). ておく = done in advance and left as is (preparation). てみる = try doing to see what happens (experimental, tentative). These are among the most frequent and most confused N4 te-form auxiliaries.

confusionGroups.name: Te-Form Direction: ていく / てくる
confusionGroups.note: Both attach to the て-form to express ongoing direction of change or movement. ていく = action moves away from the speaker's viewpoint or continues into the future. てくる = action moves toward the speaker or has been ongoing up to now. The spatial metaphor extends to abstract change over time.

confusionGroups.name: Because / Since: なので / ので
confusionGroups.note: Both express soft, objective causal reasoning (contrasting with から which is more subjective). ので follows plain verb/adjective forms; なので follows nouns and な-adjectives directly. They are near-synonyms — なので is a contraction of noun+な+ので. Both pair naturally with polite sentence endings.

confusionGroups.name: But / Contrast: けれども / しかし / でも / のに
confusionGroups.note: All express contrast or concession but differ in position, register, and emotional weight. しかし is formal sentence-initial 'however'. でも is casual sentence-initial 'but'. けれども is a clause connector (can be within or between sentences) with variants けれど/けど for casual use. のに is a clause connector expressing unexpected result with emotional undertone (frustration or surprise).

confusionGroups.name: Wonder / Rhetorical: かな / かしら / かどうか / ではないか
confusionGroups.note: All express wondering or uncertainty but differ in gender register and function. かな is neutral self-directed wondering. かしら is feminine-speech soft uncertainty. かどうか embeds a yes/no question into a larger clause (whether or not). ではないか (and its casual form じゃないか) seeks agreement from the listener, functioning as a rhetorical tag question.

confusionGroups.name: Want / Desire: てほしい / たがる / 欲しい
confusionGroups.note: All express desire but differ in subject, object type, and directional nuance. 欲しい = want to have a thing (speaker's desire for a noun, 1st person). てほしい = want someone else to do something for you (directed desire toward another's action). たがる = appears to want to do (third-person visible desire for an action, used because Japanese avoids stating others' inner feelings directly).

syn_must.label: Must (obligation synonyms)
syn_must.note: なければいけない and なければならない are interchangeable in meaning; ならない is slightly more formal/written.

syn_appearance.label: Appears / Seems (ようだ ≈ みたいだ)
syn_appearance.note: ようだ and みたいだ are largely interchangeable in meaning; みたいだ is casual spoken, ようだ is neutral-to-formal.

syn_difficulty.label: Hard to do (にくい ≈ づらい)
syn_difficulty.note: にくい and づらい both mean 'difficult to do' and are often interchangeable; づらい carries a more uncomfortable or emotionally painful nuance.

syn_but_formal.label: However (formal) (けれども ≈ しかし)
syn_but_formal.note: けれども and しかし both mean 'however/but' in formal registers and are largely interchangeable as sentence-initial connectors, though しかし is more strictly sentence-initial while けれども can connect clauses.

syn_wonder.label: I wonder (かな ≈ かしら)
syn_wonder.note: かな and かしら are both sentence-final particles for self-directed wondering; かしら is associated with feminine or older-generation speech.

if; after; when (conditional/temporal)
why don't you; how about doing
what should I do?; what would be good?
if; in the case that; if it is the case that
whenever A happens, B naturally happens; if (natural consequence)
to do something for someone (speaker or in-group acts for out-group)
to get/have someone do something for you; to receive the favor of
to do something for me/us (someone does a favor for the speaker)
to do for (casual/condescending; speaker acts for someone of lower status)
to try doing; to do and see (experiment with an action)
to do something in advance; to do and leave it that way
to finish completely; to do something by accident/regrettably
to continue doing; to go on doing; (action moves away from speaker)
to come to be; to continue; to start (action moves toward speaker or begins)
I want you to do; I want (someone else) to do
I'm glad that; it was good that (expression of relief)
I'm sorry for doing; sorry to have done
could you please do; would you mind doing (polite request)
conjunctive; so; because of (て-form as clause connector)
looks like; appears; seems (based on direct visual evidence)
I heard that; it is said that (second-hand information)
seems like; looks like (adverbial/adjectival form of そうだ)
it seems; apparently; I hear that (inference from evidence/rumor)
appears; seems; looks as if (inference based on observation)
like; as; similar to (comparison or manner)
like; similar to; resembling; it seems (casual ようだ)
like; similar to (adjectival form; modifies nouns)
like; similar to; in the same way as (adverbial form)
to look; to seem; to appear (used for visual/perceptual judgments)
causative; to make someone do; to let someone do
potential form; to be able to do; can do
causative-passive; to be made to do (by someone else)
please let me do; may I do (polite request for permission)
to be (honorific/formal equivalent of です)
to be; to exist (polite equivalent of あります)
to be; to come; to go (honorific equivalent of いる/くる/いく)
to do (honorific equivalent of する; for respected people's actions)
to do (humble equivalent of します; speaker humbles their own action)
please do (honorific request form)
to do (honorific form for describing respected person's actions)
must do; have to do (obligation)
must do; have to do (stronger formal obligation)
it must be; should be; expected to be (reasonable expectation)
cannot be; there is no way; impossible (denial of expectation)
while; during; between (for the entire duration of A)
while; during (something else happened within the duration of A)
while doing; simultaneously (two actions at once)
about to do; just started; at the point of (depending on verb form)
just finished doing; just did (immediate past)
in the process of doing; in the middle of (action ongoing right now)
as it is; without changing; leaving in the current state
by; by the time; no later than (deadline)
before doing; before a time (prior to an action or event)
more than; rather than; compared to
easy to do; prone to; tends to
difficult to do; hard to do
difficult to do; painful/uncomfortable to do (more emotional nuance than にくい)
too much; excessively; over-
called; named; that; a thing called
the fact that; means that (nominalizes a clause)
you could say; it would be fair to say
it is said that; they say that; reportedly
to reach the point that; to come to be able to; to come to do
to try to; to make sure; to make an effort to
thinking of doing; planning to; intending to
plan to; intend to; scheduled to (concrete plan)
to decide to do; to make it a rule to do (speaker's decision)
it has been decided; it turns out that (result of outside decision)
there are times when; sometimes (habitual or occasional occurrence)
to be able to do; can do (ability or possibility)
one's intention; conviction; plan to do
although; in spite of; even though (unexpected contrast)
in order to; for the purpose of; needed for
even if; even though; no matter how
whether or not; if or not
might; perhaps; may; possibly (uncertain possibility)
I wonder; I wonder if (feminine speech; soft uncertainty)
I wonder; should I; is it (self-directed wondering)
question particle (casual masculine speech)
just by doing; just with; only by
such as; things like; and so on; et cetera
or; otherwise; either...or

isn't it; right; seeking agreement or confirmation
question particle (marks sentences as questions)
to start doing; to begin doing
to finish doing; to be done doing

whether or not; if (embedded question)
surely; undoubtedly; certainly; I'm sure (speaker's strong conviction)

to notice; to realize; to become aware of
to decide on; to make something (decision or transformation)
in; inside; among; within
the reason for ~ is; it is ~ that (cleft sentence / emphasis)
do this (soft command; often used by superiors/parents)
don't do; prohibition (plain speech command not to do)
since; because (soft causal explanation; slightly more casual than ので)
because; since (objective/factual reason; softer than から)
nominalizer for adjectives; turns adjectives into nouns

around that time; approximately; about (approximate time)

but; however (sentence-initial; casual equivalent of しかし)
or something; how about; something like (mild suggestion)
but; however; although (clause connector; multiple formality variants)

to want something; desirative adjective for objects
to receive (humble); set phrase before meals
